intro: Coelorachis fasciculata is a perennial grass species commonly known as the clustered spider grass or jointed spidergrass, belonging to the Poaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Coelorachis fasciculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Coelorachis fasciculata is made into tea, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in herbal remedies and supplements to support overall health and well-being.
